exercises.title: Polymorphism: Shape Area Calculator
exercises.description: Create Circle, Rectangle, and Triangle classes each with an area() method. Write a function total_area(shapes) that works polymorphically with any shape.
exercises.expectedOutput:
Circle(r=5): area=78.54
Rectangle(4x6): area=24
Triangle(b=3, h=8): area=12.0
Total area: 114.54
hints: total_area: return sum(s.area() for s in shapes)
All three classes respond to .area() - that's polymorphism

exercises.title: Magic Methods: Vector2D
exercises.description: Implement a Vector2D class with __str__, __repr__, __add__, __sub__, and __eq__ magic methods so the class works naturally with Python operators.
exercises.starterCode:
class Vector2D:
    def __init__(self, x, y):
        self.x = x
        self.y = y

    def __str__(self):
        # TODO: Return "Vector2D(3, 4)"
        pass

    def __repr__(self):
        # TODO: Return "Vector2D(x=3, y=4)"
        pass

    def __add__(self, other):
        # TODO: Return new Vector2D(self.x + other.x, self.y + other.y)
        pass

    def __sub__(self, other):
        # TODO: Return new Vector2D(self.x - other.x, self.y - other.y)
        pass

    def __eq__(self, other):
        # TODO: Return True if x and y match
        pass

    def magnitude(self):
        # TODO: Return sqrt(x^2 + y^2)
        import math
        pass


v1 = Vector2D(3, 4)
v2 = Vector2D(1, 2)

print(str(v1))
print(repr(v1))
print(v1 + v2)
print(v1 - v2)
print(v1 == Vector2D(3, 4))
print(v1 == v2)
print(round(v1.magnitude(), 2))
exercises.expectedOutput:
Vector2D(3, 4)
Vector2D(x=3, y=4)
Vector2D(4, 6)
Vector2D(2, 2)
True
False
5.0
hints: __eq__: return self.x == other.x and self.y == other.y
magnitude: import math; return math.sqrt(self.x**2 + self.y**2)

exercises.title: Container Magic Methods: Playlist
exercises.description: Implement __len__, __contains__, __iter__, and __str__ on a Playlist class to make it behave like a container.
exercises.starterCode:
class Playlist:
    def __init__(self, name):
        self.name = name
        self._songs = []

    def add(self, song):
        self._songs.append(song)

    def __len__(self):
        # TODO: Return number of songs
        pass

    def __contains__(self, song):
        # TODO: Return True if song is in self._songs
        pass

    def __iter__(self):
        # TODO: Return iter(self._songs)
        pass

    def __str__(self):
        # TODO: Return: "Playlist 'My Mix' (3 songs)"
        pass


p = Playlist("My Mix")
p.add("Song A")
p.add("Song B")
p.add("Song C")

print(len(p))
print("Song B" in p)
print("Song D" in p)

for song in p:
    print(f"  Playing: {song}")

print(str(p))
exercises.expectedOutput:
3
True
False
  Playing: Song A
  Playing: Song B
  Playing: Song C
Playlist 'My Mix' (3 songs)

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Duck Typing
warmup.description: What does this code print? Notice make_sound works on any object with a .speak() method, regardless of type.
